title: RAG Security: Poisoning, Injection, Authorization, and Unsafe Effects · Language Clinic: Rag Security Threat Model
summary: A shared knowledge base permits uploads, embeds hidden instructions, filters tenant results after retrieval, forwards generated URLs to tools, and assumes grounding reduces security risk. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the security threat model increment in RAG threat model and adversarial test suite. Learn…
objectives: Model actors, assets, trust boundaries, connectors, parsers, embeddings, indexes, queries, context, models, outputs, tools, administration, and recovery.
Separate development, validation, test, canary, and incident sets and track document, query, judgment, prompt, model, and result versions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern

Build deterministic Python evidence to contain poisoning, embedding manipulation, indirect injection, tenant leaks, unsafe output, and tool effects; preserve source provenance, tenant authorization, deletion, freshness, citation offsets, and untrusted-data separation; then double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope. A defensible solution applies Rag Security Threat Model, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to RAG threat model and adversarial test suite.

content.code:
# Worked focus: rag-security-threat-model — Model actors, assets, trust boundaries, connectors, parsers, embeddings, indexes, queries, context, models, outputs, tools, administration, and recovery.
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-security-poisoning-injection-3
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-security-poisoning-injection-3.
# Case 5798: a multimodal maintenance-manual release.
# Operating constraint: retain corpus, document, chunk, model, index, prompt, query, and evaluation version identity.
# Changed case: move the only supporting passage to the middle of the context and rerun the position slice.
def worked_rag_security_poisoning_injection_3(documents: int = 20, quarantined: int = 2, tenant_authorized: bool = True, retrieved_instructions_ignored: bool = True, output_validated: bool = True, tools_enabled: bool = False) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "corpusworked3",
        "query3dekrow",
        "tenant5798",
        "indexworked35798",
        "prompt3dekrow5798",
        "evaluation5798worked3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if documents <= 0 or quarantined < 0 or quarantined > documents:
        return False, quarantined, "ingestion-security"
    if not tenant_authorized:
        return False, quarantined, "retrieval-authorization"
    if not retrieved_instructions_ignored:
        return False, quarantined, "indirect-injection"
    if not output_validated:
        return False, quarantined, "unsafe-output"
    if tools_enabled:
        return False, quarantined, "tool-separation"
    assert quarantined <= documents
    return True, documents - quarantined, "defended-corpus"

steps.title: RAG and Python Defend the Rag Security Threat Model model · Theory Rag Security Threat Model
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Rag Security Threat Model: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory RAG engineering team handles case 3757 during a regulated document deletion request. Build deterministic Python evidence to contain poisoning, embedding manipulation, indirect injection, tenant leaks, unsafe output, and tool effects; retain corpus, document, chunk, model, index, prompt, query, and evaluation version identity; then insert an untrusted instruction into a relevant document and prove it remains data. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Model actors, assets, trust boundaries, connectors, parsers, embeddings, indexes, queries, context, models, outputs, tools, administration, and recovery.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Defend the Rag Security Threat Model model · Theory Rag Security Threat Model.
steps.why: Retrieving Rag Security Threat Model through the concrete RAG threat model and adversarial test suite case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Security Threat Model model · Theory Rag Security Threat Model, keep Rag Security Threat Model connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Model actors, assets, trust boundaries, connectors, parsers, embeddings, indexes, queries, context, models, outputs, tools, administration, and recovery.

hints: For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Security Threat Model model · Theory Rag Security Threat Model, name the stakeholder, corpus, source, document, chunk, model, index, query, relevance, tenant, context, citation, evaluation, and transfer boundary, Rag Security Threat Model precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RAG and Python Defend the Rag Security Threat Model model · Theory Rag Security Threat Model, trace this concrete input and predict ingestion, normalization, retrieval, filter, fusion, reranking, context, claim, citation, abstention, latency, cost, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory RAG engineering team handles case 3757 during a regulated document deletion request. Build deterministic Python evidence to contain poisoning, embedding manipulation, indirect injection, tenant leaks, unsafe output, and tool effects; retain corpus, document, chunk, model, index, prompt, query, and evaluation version identity; then insert an untrusted instruction into a relevant document and prove it remains data. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Model actors, assets, trust boundaries, connectors, parsers, embeddings, indexes, queries, context, models, outputs, tools, administration, and recovery.

For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Security Threat Model model · Theory Rag Security Threat Model, challenge the documented misconception with changed-query, changed-corpus, relevance, retrieval, grounding, citation, adversarial, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native or production-transfer evidence: RAG security is the same as protecting the model API key.
